shortDescription:
DUN-TFC-TDF-W-SUBV1-CIRM : subband spectrogram-based dense U-Net with time-distributed FCs and complex ideal ratio mask

Architectural specifications :
- W-variant from vanilla DUN-TFC-TDF : shared channels into one U-Net instead of stem-wise U-Nets
- 9 blocks of 3 internal layers with identity as last activation
- Internal 4-subband processing : the input is chunked along the frequency axis, the resulting chunks are stacked along the channel axis
- cIRM module (see ByteDance's paper for details on this particular implementation)
- First and last convolutional blocks are pointwise blocks (do not handle the last frequency bin anymore)
- 6 stems prediction : drums / bass / other / vocals / piano / guitar
- Activation is ReLU in TFC-TDF blocks with bias enabled
- All other parameters are default

Training specifications :
The model has been trained on UPMS-WAV (stem-complete cleansubset), YousingPlus & MusDB train & validation sets (sr=44100Hz).
Batches of size 4 of 3s audio clips randomly mixed with Adam optimizer.
SI-SNR MusDB test set : 4.8dB, epoch 1,514.

Inference tips :
1. Load stereo waveform
2. Generate STFT and stack real and imaginary channels
3. Feed in the model
4. Do the iSTFT

An overlap may be needed for small slices where outer bins are discarded.
